name: @kolm/vision-tokenize-worker
description: Isolated kolm vision tokenizer worker. Tokenizes raw image bytes into CLIP / SigLIP patch-embedding token arrays for the W771 VLM distill trainer. Heavy ML deps (transformers + torch + Pillow) live OUTSIDE this Node worker, in an opt-in Python process. Root kolm install stays light. When the Python toolchain or the requested CLIPProcessor model is not on disk the worker emits an honest no_detector_installed envelope -- it NEVER silently invents patch token counts.

processors.note: Default processor is CLIPProcessor('openai/clip-vit-large-patch14'): 224x224 input, 14x14 patch grid -> 256 patch tokens (plus a CLS token at index 0 -> 257 sequence positions, hidden_size 1024). Without `transformers` + `torch` + `Pillow` installed Python-side (or a tokenizer wired via $KOLM_VISION_TOKENIZE_CMD), the worker returns {ok:false, error:'no_detector_installed', install_hint, patch_token_count:null}. Install path: pip install transformers torch Pillow; OR set $KOLM_VISION_TOKENIZE_CMD to a binary that accepts --path <file> --model <name> --json and emits the W771b envelope on stdout.
